title: TermDraw-Bench
summary: Benchmark for ASCII diagram generation and editing.

categories.name: Box Layout Basics
categories.description: Single boxes, rows, columns, arrows, lanes, and spacing-sensitive ASCII layout tasks.

tasks.title: Single labeled box
tasks.prompt: Draw a single ASCII box with the label "API Gateway" centered inside it.

tasks.title: Decision diamond
tasks.prompt: Draw a flowchart decision diamond labeled "Is auth?" with two exit paths: a "Yes" path going right to a box labeled "Dashboard", and a "No" path going down to a box labeled "Login Page". Use / and \ characters to form the diamond shape.

tasks.title: Multi-line box content
tasks.prompt: Draw a single ASCII box labeled "Config" containing two lines of content: "host: localhost" on the first line and "port: 8080" on the second line.

tasks.title: L-shape layout
tasks.prompt: Draw three boxes in an L-shape: "Node A" on the top-left, "Node B" to the right of Node A, and "Node C" below Node B. Connect A to B with a right arrow, and B to C with a downward arrow.

tasks.title: Title bar box
tasks.prompt: Draw an ASCII box with a title bar. Use "=" as the divider between the title and content. Title: "Server Status". Content: "Running".

tasks.title: Labeled bidirectional arrow
tasks.prompt: Draw two boxes "Client" and "Server" side by side. Between them show two arrows: one going right labeled "request" and one going left labeled "response".

tasks.title: Circle node and box
tasks.prompt: Draw a circle node labeled "Start" using ( ) notation, connected with a right arrow to a rectangular box labeled "Process".

tasks.title: Horizontal chain
tasks.prompt: Draw three boxes labeled "Input", "Process", "Output" connected left-to-right with arrows.

tasks.title: Vertical stack
tasks.prompt: Draw four boxes stacked vertically: "Load Balancer", "App Server", "Cache", "Database". Connect them top-to-bottom with downward arrows.

tasks.title: Two-column layout
tasks.prompt: Draw a two-column layout. Left column: one box labeled "Client". Right column: two stacked boxes labeled "Primary DB" on top and "Replica DB" below. Connect the Client to both DB boxes with arrows pointing right.

tasks.title: Bidirectional arrows
tasks.prompt: Draw two boxes "Service A" and "Service B" side by side with a bidirectional arrow between them showing data flows both ways.

tasks.title: Labeled arrow
tasks.prompt: Draw two boxes "Producer" and "Consumer" connected by a right-pointing arrow. Place the label "events" above the arrow line between the two boxes.

tasks.title: Fan-out
tasks.prompt: Draw one box "API Server" at the top. Below it, draw three boxes in a row: "Users DB", "Products DB", "Orders DB". Connect the API Server to each of the three boxes with downward arrows.

tasks.title: Fan-in
tasks.prompt: Draw three boxes in a row at the top: "Web", "Mobile", "CLI". Below them, draw one box "GraphQL Gateway". Connect each of the three top boxes to the Gateway with downward arrows.

tasks.title: Multi-section box
tasks.prompt: Draw an ASCII box representing a database table called "users". Use a horizontal divider line inside the box to separate the table name (top section) from three fields listed below it: "id (PK)", "email", "created_at".

categories.name: Network Topology Diagrams
categories.description: Distributed systems, clusters, buses, data flows, and topology-heavy connection diagrams.

tasks.title: Client-server-database chain
tasks.prompt: Draw a client-server-database chain: "Browser" → "Web Server" → "PostgreSQL". Show arrows between each.

tasks.title: DiLoCo training
tasks.prompt: Draw a DiLoCo training diagram. Four "Worker" boxes in a row, each connected downward to a local "Inner Optimizer" box. One "Outer Optimizer" box above all workers, connected to each worker with dashed arrows (- - >). Label the dashed arrows "outer loop sync".

tasks.title: Ring allreduce
tasks.prompt: Draw a ring allreduce topology. Four GPU nodes in a directed ring: "GPU-0" → "GPU-1" → "GPU-2" → "GPU-3" → "GPU-0". All arrows go clockwise. Label each arrow "gradient".

tasks.title: Peer discovery
tasks.prompt: Draw a peer discovery diagram. Six Pi nodes in two rows of three: top row "Pi-1", "Pi-2", "Pi-3"; bottom row "Pi-4", "Pi-5", "Pi-6". Show dotted broadcast lines (using ...) between all Pi nodes representing mDNS multicast. One "Tracker" node to the side connected only to Pi-1.

tasks.title: Parameter server
tasks.prompt: Draw a parameter server architecture. One "Parameter Server" at the top. Four "Worker" boxes below it. Show bidirectional arrows between PS and each worker labeled "push gradients" (Worker→PS) and "pull params" (PS→Worker).
tasks.preview:
+------------------+
                         | Parameter Server |
                         +------------------+
                    pull params to all workers
               /             |             |             \
              v              v             v              v

tasks.title: Primary-replica DB
tasks.prompt: Draw a primary-replica database setup. One "Primary DB" on the left. Three replicas to the right: "Replica-1", "Replica-2", "Replica-3". Show arrows from Primary to each Replica labeled "async sync".

tasks.title: Service mesh with sidecars
tasks.prompt: Draw a service mesh with sidecars. Three services in a row: "Service A", "Service B", "Service C". Each has a small sidecar box directly below it labeled "Proxy". All Proxy boxes connect to a central "Control Plane" box below.

tasks.title: CDN architecture
tasks.prompt: Draw a CDN architecture. One "Origin Server" on the right. Two edge cache nodes: "Edge-US" and "Edge-EU". "Users (Global)" on the left connecting to both edge nodes. Edge nodes connect to Origin on cache miss. Label edge-to-origin arrows "cache miss".

tasks.title: Pi PoE cluster
tasks.prompt: Draw a Raspberry Pi PoE cluster. "Internet" at top connects to "Router". Router connects to "TP-Link Switch". Switch connects to four Pi nodes: "Pi-1", "Pi-2", "Pi-3", "Pi-4".

tasks.title: VPN tunnel
tasks.prompt: Draw a VPN tunnel. "Client" on the left connects through an "[Encrypted Tunnel]" annotation to "VPN Server" in the middle. VPN Server connects to "Internet" on the right.

tasks.title: DNS resolution
tasks.prompt: Draw DNS resolution. "Browser" → "Local Resolver" → "Root NS" → "TLD NS (.com)" → "Auth NS (example.com)" → "IP Address returned". Linear chain left to right.

tasks.title: NGINX load balancer
tasks.prompt: Draw a load balancer "NGINX" receiving traffic from "Client" and distributing to three backends: "App-1", "App-2", "App-3".

tasks.title: TCP three-way handshake
tasks.prompt: Draw a TCP three-way handshake between "Client" and "Server" using a sequence diagram (vertical lanes). Show: Client sends SYN →, Server replies ← SYN-ACK, Client sends ACK →.

tasks.title: Event-driven system
tasks.prompt: Draw an event-driven system. Three producers: "Service A", "Service B", "Service C". All publish to "Event Bus". Two consumers subscribe from Event Bus: "Analytics Service", "Audit Service".

tasks.title: Federated learning
tasks.prompt: Draw a federated learning setup. One "Central Aggregator" in the middle. Four local trainers around it: "Trainer-1", "Trainer-2", "Trainer-3", "Trainer-4". Bidirectional arrows between Aggregator and each Trainer labeled "model update".

tasks.title: Speculative decoding
tasks.prompt: Draw speculative decoding. "Draft Model (small)" generates tokens passed to "Verifier Model (large)". Show two output paths: "accept" going to "Output" and "reject" looping back to Draft Model.

tasks.title: PagedAttention KV cache
tasks.prompt: Draw PagedAttention KV cache. Show "Logical Blocks" on the left (Block 0, Block 1, Block 2) mapped via arrows to "Physical Blocks" on the right (Physical 3, Physical 0, Physical 7). Add a "Block Table" box in the middle showing the mapping.

tasks.title: FSDP sharding
tasks.prompt: Draw FSDP sharding. Four GPU boxes: "GPU-0 (Shard 0)", "GPU-1 (Shard 1)", "GPU-2 (Shard 2)", "GPU-3 (Shard 3)". Show AllGather arrows between all GPUs (each to each) labeled "allgather".

tasks.title: CI/CD chain
tasks.prompt: Draw a CI/CD pipeline as a left-to-right chain: "Code Push" → "Build" → "Unit Tests" → "Integration Tests" → "Staging" → "Production".

tasks.title: API gateway pattern
tasks.prompt: Draw an API gateway pattern. "Client" connects to "API Gateway". Inside the gateway show "Auth" and "Rate Limiter" as sub-components. Gateway routes to three services: "User Service", "Order Service", "Product Service".

tasks.title: Kubernetes sidecar pattern
tasks.prompt: Draw a Kubernetes sidecar pattern. One "Pod" box containing two sub-boxes side by side: "App Container" and "Proxy Sidecar". The proxy connects to "Service Mesh" outside the pod.

tasks.title: Paxos cluster
tasks.prompt: Draw a 3-node Paxos cluster. Show "Proposer" sending "Prepare(n)" to three "Acceptor" boxes. Acceptors reply "Promise(n)" back to Proposer. Then Proposer sends "Accept(n,v)" to Acceptors.

tasks.title: Raft cluster
tasks.prompt: Draw a Raft consensus cluster with 5 nodes. One is labeled "Leader". The others are "Follower-1", "Follower-2", "Follower-3", "Follower-4". Show heartbeat arrows from Leader to each Follower only (not the reverse).

tasks.title: Smolhive topology
tasks.prompt: Draw a full smolhive topology. One "Mac Mini M4 (Trainer)" at the top. Below it, four "Jetson Orin Nano" boxes and two "Raspberry Pi" boxes. Trainer connects to all six. Jetsons and Pis connect to each other with dashed "P2P sync" arrows.

tasks.title: Ring network
tasks.prompt: Draw a ring network with 4 nodes: "Node-A", "Node-B", "Node-C", "Node-D". Connect them in a ring: A→B→C→D→A.

tasks.title: Kubernetes cluster
tasks.prompt: Draw a Kubernetes cluster. One outer box labeled "Control Plane" contains three sub-components: "API Server", "etcd", "Scheduler". Below the Control Plane, three worker boxes: "Worker-1", "Worker-2", "Worker-3". Connect Control Plane to each Worker.

tasks.title: Pub/sub
tasks.prompt: Draw a pub/sub diagram. "Order Service" publishes to "Kafka". "Inventory Service" and "Notification Service" both subscribe from Kafka. Label the arrow from Order Service to Kafka "publish". Label arrows from Kafka to subscribers "subscribe".
tasks.preview:
Order Service
+---------------+
| Order Service |
+---------------+
     | publish
     v

tasks.title: Home ML lab cluster
tasks.prompt: Draw a home ML lab cluster. One "Mac Mini M4 (Orchestrator)" at the top connected via "Thunderbolt" to three workers: "Mac Mini M4 Worker-1", "Mac Mini M4 Worker-2", "Mac Mini M4 Worker-3". Also show a "NAS" box connected only to the Orchestrator.

tasks.title: Training to inference nodes
tasks.prompt: Draw a diagram with "Mac Mini M4 (Training)" at the top connected via "SSH / TCP" to four inference nodes in a row: "Jetson Orin Nano-1", "Jetson Orin Nano-2", "Jetson Orin Nano-3", "Jetson Orin Nano-4".

tasks.title: BitTorrent swarm
tasks.prompt: Draw a BitTorrent swarm. One "Tracker" connects to 5 peers: "Peer-A", "Peer-B", "Peer-C", "Peer-D", "Peer-E". Additionally show peer-to-peer connections: A-B, B-C, C-D, D-E, and A-E.

categories.name: Diagram Editing
categories.description: Image-to-text edit tasks where a model must transform an existing ASCII diagram correctly.

tasks.title: Add a node
tasks.prompt: Add a "Database" box to the right of the Server box. Connect Server to Database with a right-pointing arrow. Do not change anything else.

tasks.title: Merge two services
tasks.prompt: Merge the "Auth" and "Session" boxes into one box labeled "Auth+Session Service". Update all connections so the merged box connects to both "Users DB" and "Sessions DB".

tasks.title: Add follower to Raft cluster
tasks.prompt: Add a "Follower-5" node connected from the Leader.

tasks.title: Label arrows
tasks.prompt: Add the label "HTTP" to the first arrow, "gRPC" to the second, "SQL" to the third, "cache" to the fourth.

tasks.title: Swap node positions
tasks.prompt: Swap positions: move Node A to the right and Node B to the left. Update the arrow direction accordingly.

tasks.title: Add grouping box
tasks.prompt: Draw an outer grouping box around all three labeled "Region: EU". Do not modify the inner boxes.

tasks.title: Remove follower
tasks.prompt: Remove Follower-3 and its connection from the Leader.

tasks.title: Add sidecars
tasks.prompt: Add a sidecar "Proxy" box directly below each app box. Connect each Proxy to "Mesh Control Plane" below.

tasks.title: Bidirectional conversion
tasks.prompt: Change all three arrows to bidirectional.

tasks.title: Swap B and C
tasks.prompt: Swap the positions of B and C. Update arrows accordingly: A→C→B→D.

tasks.title: Add version annotations
tasks.prompt: Add a version annotation inside each box on a new line: "v2.1", "v1.8", "v3.0" respectively.

tasks.title: Remove a node
tasks.prompt: Remove the Cache box and its connections. Connect Web directly to DB with an arrow.

tasks.title: Split database node
tasks.prompt: Split into two boxes side by side: "Write Primary" on the left and "Read Replica" on the right. Show an arrow from Write Primary to Read Replica labeled "replication".

tasks.title: Rename a node
tasks.prompt: Rename "App Srv" to "FastAPI". Resize the box to fit the new label. Keep everything else exactly the same.

tasks.title: Add an edge
tasks.prompt: Add a bidirectional arrow between Service and Monitor labeled "metrics" above the arrow line.

tasks.title: Add internal section
tasks.prompt: Add an internal section below the title. Use a horizontal divider (+-...+) to separate. Add two endpoints below the divider: "/login" and "/refresh".

tasks.title: Scale up
tasks.prompt: Scale the app layer to 3 instances. Add "App-2" and "App-3" alongside App-1. Connect NGINX to all three with arrows.

tasks.title: Insert intermediary
tasks.prompt: Insert an "API Server" between Browser and Database. Remove the direct Browser-to-Database arrow. Add Browser→API Server and API Server→Database arrows.

tasks.title: Reverse arrow direction
tasks.prompt: Reverse the arrow direction. It should now point from NodeB to NodeA.

tasks.title: Mark failure node
tasks.prompt: Mark the Server as a failure point: change its corner characters from + to * and add the label "FAILED" directly below the Server box.

categories.name: Software Architecture Diagrams
categories.description: Canonical architecture sketches such as gateways, pipelines, feeds, rate limiters, and storage systems.

tasks.title: 3-tier web architecture
tasks.prompt:
Draw the classic 3-tier web architecture:
- "Client (Browser)" on the left
- "Load Balancer (NGINX)" in the middle receiving traffic from Client
- Three app servers in a column: "App-1", "App-2", "App-3" receiving traffic from LB
- "PostgreSQL" database on the right receiving queries from all app servers
Show arrows between each tier.

tasks.title: Search autocomplete
tasks.prompt:
Draw a search autocomplete system:
- "User" types into "Search API"
- Search API checks "Trie Cache (In-Memory)" first
- On miss, Search API queries "Prefix Index (Elasticsearch)"
- "Data Aggregation Service" reads "Query Logs DB" and rebuilds both the Trie Cache and Prefix Index periodically
- Results flow back from Search API to User

tasks.title: Twitter news feed hybrid fan-out
tasks.prompt:
Draw the Twitter news feed architecture with hybrid fan-out:
- "User" posts via "Tweet Service" which writes to "Tweets DB (Cassandra)"
- Tweet Service publishes to "Fan-out Service" via "Kafka"
- Fan-out Service reads "Social Graph DB" to find followers
- Fan-out Service writes to "Feed Cache (Redis)" for normal users — label this path "fan-out on write"
- For celebrity accounts: "Feed Service" reads directly from Tweets DB — label this path "fan-out on read"
- User reads their timeline via "Feed Service" which checks Feed Cache first

tasks.title: Video upload pipeline
tasks.prompt:
Draw a video upload and streaming pipeline:
- "User" uploads raw video to "Upload Service"
- Upload Service stores in "Raw Storage (S3)" and queues a job in "Transcoding Queue (Kafka)"
- Three "Transcoder Worker" boxes consume from Kafka and write output to "Processed Storage (S3)"
- "CDN" pulls from Processed Storage and serves video to User
- "Metadata Service" updates "Video DB" at each processing stage

tasks.title: Chat system
tasks.prompt:
Draw a real-time chat system:
- "User A" and "User B" each connected to "Chat Server" via WebSocket (label connections "WebSocket")
- Chat Server publishes to "Message Queue (Kafka)"
- "Message Service" consumes from Kafka and writes to "Messages DB (Cassandra)"
- "Presence Service" tracks online status in "Presence Cache (Redis)"
- Chat Server checks Presence Service before routing
- For offline users: "Push Notification Service" sends via "APNs / FCM"

tasks.title: Ride-sharing backend
tasks.prompt:
Draw a ride-sharing backend (Uber-style):
- "Rider App" and "Driver App" both connect to "API Gateway"
- "Location Service" receives GPS pings from Driver App via WebSocket, stores in "Location Cache (Redis)" with geohash label
- "Matching Service" reads from Location Cache to find nearby drivers, creates trips in "Trips DB"
- "Payment Service" processes payments, writes to "Payments DB" and external "Stripe"
- "Notification Service" sends status updates to both apps

tasks.title: Lambda architecture
tasks.prompt:
Draw a Lambda architecture data pipeline:
- Three data sources publish to "Kafka (Event Stream)": "App Events", "DB Change Stream", "IoT Sensors"
- Speed layer: Kafka → "Stream Processor (Flink)" → "Time-Series DB" — label "speed layer"
- Batch layer: Kafka → "Batch Processor (Spark)" → "Data Warehouse (BigQuery)" — label "batch layer"
- Both outputs feed "Analytics Dashboard"
- "Data Catalog" connects to both storage layers

tasks.title: Read replica pattern
tasks.prompt:
Draw a database read replica pattern:
- "App Server" on the left
- "Primary DB" in the middle — App Server writes to it (label arrow "write")
- "Read Replica-1" and "Read Replica-2" to the right — App Server reads from both (label arrows "read")
- Primary DB replicates to both replicas with dashed arrows labeled "async replication"

tasks.title: CI/CD pipeline
tasks.prompt:
Draw a CI/CD pipeline as a left-to-right chain with these stages in order:
"Code Push" → "Build" → "Unit Tests" → "Integration Tests" → "Staging Deploy" → "Prod Deploy"
Also show a dashed "rollback" arrow from "Prod Deploy" back to "Staging Deploy" labeled "on failure".

tasks.title: CDN architecture
tasks.prompt:
Draw a CDN architecture:
- "Users (Global)" on the left
- Two edge cache nodes in the middle: "Edge Cache (US)" and "Edge Cache (EU)"
- "Origin Server" on the right
- Users connect to both edge nodes (label "cached response")
- Both edge nodes connect to Origin Server (label "cache miss")

tasks.title: URL shortener architecture
tasks.prompt:
Draw a URL shortener system architecture:
- "Client" → "Load Balancer" → two "App Server" boxes
- App Servers check "Redis Cache" first (label arrow "cache hit / O(1)")
- On cache miss, App Servers query "Cassandra DB" (label arrow "cache miss")
- App Servers write click events to "Kafka"
- "Analytics Service" consumes from Kafka

tasks.title: Rate limiter
tasks.prompt:
Draw a rate limiter architecture:
- "Client" → "Rate Limiter" service
- Rate Limiter checks "Redis (token bucket counters)"
- Allowed requests: Rate Limiter → "App Server"
- Rejected requests: Rate Limiter → Client with dashed arrow labeled "429 Too Many Requests"

tasks.title: Notification system
tasks.prompt:
Draw a notification system:
- Three event sources publish to "Kafka": "Order Service", "Payment Service", "Shipping Service"
- "Notification Service" consumes from Kafka and checks "User Preferences DB"
- Notification Service delivers to three channels: "Email Service", "SMS Service", "Push Service"
- Each channel connects to its provider: Email → "SendGrid", SMS → "Twilio", Push → "FCM"

tasks.title: Microservices API gateway
tasks.prompt:
Draw a microservices API gateway pattern:
- "Mobile Client" and "Web Client" both connect to "API Gateway"
- Inside the API Gateway box, show two sub-components: "Auth Middleware" and "Rate Limiter"
- Gateway routes to three backend services, each with its own DB:
  - "User Service" with "Users DB"
  - "Product Service" with "Products DB"
  - "Order Service" with "Orders DB"

tasks.title: Dropbox-style file storage
tasks.prompt:
Draw a Dropbox-style file storage system:
- "Client (Desktop)" uploads to "Upload Service"
- Upload Service stores file chunks in "S3 (Block Store)" and metadata in "Metadata DB (PostgreSQL)"
- "Download Service" reads from both S3 and Metadata DB to serve files to Client
- "Sync Service" connects to "Notification Queue (Kafka)" to send change notifications to Client
